oneOf.description: A newer request arrived on the same slot.
Reason added by a newer hub.

CapabilitiesResponse.description:
`GET /system/capabilities` — what this installation can do
(hardware tier §3, available features).

api_version.description: API version served by this hub (`/api/v1`).

features.description:
Available feature flags (`asr`, `voice_cloning`, `embeddings`…).
Free-form strings: features appear with releases without a contract
change; clients test membership, never enumerate.

oneOf.description: Input-topic message (sensor sample or target patch).

CommitMethod.description:
How a selection was committed.

Unknown methods deserialize as [`CommitMethod::Unknown`] (forward
compatibility — clients ignore what they don't know).

oneOf.description: Dwell timer completed on a fixation.
Method added by a newer hub.

ConsentResponse.description:
`POST /asr/consent` — obtain a consent token via an explicit UI action.
The action is journaled locally (who, when, which device).

expires_at.description: When this token stops being accepted.

DeviceKind.description:
Kind of device requesting pairing — drives naming and display in the
caregiver space. Unknown kinds are tolerated.

oneOf.description: Kind added by a newer client.

caret.description:
Caret position, in Unicode scalar values (not bytes), `0 ≤ caret ≤
text.chars().count()`.

text.description: Current draft text. **P0 content** — never logged.

ErrorCode.description:
Stable error codes (wire: `snake_case`).

Marked `non_exhaustive` and tolerant on input ([`ErrorCode::Unknown`]):
the catalogue grows; clients fall back on `status` for codes they do not
know.

oneOf.description: Pairing attempted outside an explicitly opened window (SPEC §2.A).
Too many attempts; retry later (always carried by a 429).
No `X-Fluence-Token` header on a protected route.
Token scope does not allow this route (SPEC §2.A scope table).
Request shape was valid JSON but violated an invariant.
ASR control requires a valid consent token (SPEC §5.A).

The serving worker is down and no fallback applies. Routes with a
fallback (n-gram, OS voice) degrade instead of erroring (SPEC §2.C).
Unexpected internal error (carries no internals in `detail`).
Code added by a newer hub; clients fall back on `status`.

items.description: Items the semantic search proposes to forget.

ForgetRequest.description:
`POST /memory/forget` request — semantic forgetting (SPEC §5.B).

Two-step flow: this returns candidate items; actual deletion goes
through `DELETE /memory/items/{id}` after user confirmation. The
forget journal records **metadata only**, never the forgotten content.

about.description:
What to forget, in the user's words (« tout ce qui concerne X »).
**P0.**

oneOf.description: 8 GB RAM, no GPU (FLU-REF-1) — latency budgets are defined here.

HeadPose.description:
Head pose in degrees. Free-range (sensor-dependent); fusion treats it as
a relative offset signal (« regard désigne, tête affine », SPEC §4.C).

pitch.description: Rotation around the lateral axis, degrees.

roll.description: Rotation around the frontal axis, degrees.

yaw.description: Rotation around the vertical axis, degrees.

HealthResponse.description:
`GET /system/health` — worker states, loaded models, rolling latencies
(SPEC §5.A, D-3.3).

InputClientMessage.description:
Client → hub messages on the `input` topic: sensor samples from remote
clients (e.g. a tablet running `MediaPipe` in-browser, SPEC §4.A) and
target patches from UIs. Hub-managed drivers bypass this entirely
(in-process direct path).

oneOf.description: French frequency layout (for switch scanning, where order = speed).

target_scale.description:
Target-size multiplier applied on top of the noise-model sizing
(§4.C); `None` = automatic.

LatencyClass.description: Latency classes with contractual budgets (SPEC §5.A table).

oneOf.description: `next-chars` on a warm KV (budget p50 20 ms / p95 50 ms).
Turn ingestion incl. background KV re-warm (100 / 250 ms).

LatencyStat.description: Rolling latency statistics for one request class.

MemoryAcl.description:
Who may see/edit an item (SPEC §5.B). The caregiver space **never**
sees `private` items, even as admin.

oneOf.description: Visible to the user only (the default — intimacy first).
Caregivers may see and edit it.

last_used_at.description: Last retrieval-injection time, if ever used.

MemoryKind.description: Kind of memory item (SPEC §5.B data model).

oneOf.description: A person (« Marie, ma fille — vient le mardi »).
A recurring phrase — also a zero-latency suggestion cache (§5.B).
A summary persisted after raw-conversation purge (30 days).

items.description:
Matching items, best first (hybrid BM25 + vector, RRF fusion §5.B),
filtered by the caller's ACL.

oneOf.description: Entered by the user (or caregiver per ACL) in « Ma mémoire ».
Extracted by the post-conversation job, then user-confirmed.
Imported from contacts (vCard/CSV) or calendar (ICS), locally.

ModelId.description: A model from the registry (SPEC D-3.2).

ModelRef.description:
A model from the registry, pinned by version (D-3.2: a model never
changes silently).

NextCharsResponse.description:
`GET /sessions/{id}/next-chars?prefix=` response — next-character
distribution on the warm KV (SPEC §5.A). Never triggers a full
generation; feeds adaptive dwell, weighted scanning and Dasher.

dist.description: Character probabilities, descending. BPE→char aggregation hub-side.

PairInfo.description: `GET /pair/info` — what a pairing screen needs to display (no token).

api_version.description: API version served by this hub.

ca_fingerprint.description: Local CA fingerprint to compare on the pairing screen (TOFU path).

pairing_open.description: Whether a pairing window is currently open.

PairRequest.description: `POST /pair` request (no token; only valid while the window is open).

code.description: 8-digit single-use code shown on the main screen.

device_kind.description: What kind of device this is.

device_name.description: Human name for the device (« Tablette du lit »).

PairResponse.description: `POST /pair` response: everything the device needs to talk to the hub.

ca_cert.description: Local CA certificate (PEM) for TLS pinning (home mode, §2.A).

scope.description: Scope granted by this pairing window.

PendingMemoryItem.description: A candidate item in the validation queue.

proposed_at.description: When the extraction job proposed it.

reason.description:
Why it was proposed (« phrase répétée 3× cette semaine »). Shown to
the user; stored encrypted like everything else. **P0.**

PendingResponse.description:
`GET /memory/pending` response — the validation queue (SPEC §5.B):
candidates extracted after conversations, awaiting accept/reject.

PointerSample.description:
One pointing sample from a source, at the sensor's native rate
(30–120 Hz). Coordinates are normalized to the surface (SPEC §4.A).

Wire example (SPEC §4.A):
`{"k":"ptr","t":123456789,"src":"gaze:webcam0","x":0.41,"y":0.77,"conf":0.86,"pose":{...}}`

conf.description: Source confidence in `[0, 1]` (drives fusion weighting, SPEC §4.C).

pose.description: Head pose, when the source provides one.

t.description: Source timestamp, microseconds, monotonic per source.

x.description: Horizontal position in `[0, 1]`, relative to the surface.

y.description: Vertical position in `[0, 1]`, relative to the surface.

Problem.description:
RFC 9457 problem document, extended with [`ErrorCode`].

The `type` URI is derived from the code (`urn:fluence:problem:<code>`):
a stable URN that does not depend on owning a domain yet; it may become
a resolvable URL later without changing the codes.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code (the catalogue clients switch on).

instance.description: URI identifying this occurrence (e.g. the request path).

title.description: Short human-readable summary, stable per code (English).

id.description: Stable id (`default` for the primary profile).

ProfileId.description: A user profile (style, keyboards, modalities, voice — SPEC §5.A).

ProsodyTag.description:
Prosody tags v1 (D-6.3). Piper realizes them as rate/pitch/volume
presets; F5-TTS uses the person's own emotional reference recordings.

Wire names are English (code convention); UIs label them in the user's
language (`question` → « Question », `tenderness` → « Tendresse »…).

Rect.description:
Axis-aligned rectangle in viewport pixels, serialized as `[x, y, w, h]`
(SPEC §4.A wire format).

Validation: all components finite, `w ≥ 0`, `h ≥ 0` (`x`/`y` may be
negative — a target can hang off-surface while scrolling).

Scope.description: Access scope of a device token (SPEC §2.A scope table).

oneOf.description: Text to display, speech state — read only (partner-facing screen).
Full composer, suggestions, TTS, input (the user's devices).
Everything (embedded UI and local CLI only).

SelectionEvent.description:
Hub → UI selection events on the `input` topic (SPEC §4.A).

Budget: a committed selection reaches the UI < 20 ms after the decision.

Marked `non_exhaustive`: event enums grow over time; clients must
tolerate (ignore) unknown events rather than fail.

oneOf.description: The selection engine's focus entered a target.
Dwell progress on the focused target (drives the UI gauge).

eta_ms.description: Estimated remaining time to commit, milliseconds.

oneOf.description: Focus/dwell was cancelled (target left, signal lost…).
Scanning highlight moved to a group (SPEC §4.A).

group.description: Highlighted group, by convention `row:<n>` / `col:<n>` / target id.

SessionId.description: A conversation session with a warm KV-cache hub-side (SPEC §5.A).

SlotId.description:
Cancellation slot for `/suggest`: a new request on the same slot
aborts the previous one (SPEC §5.A).

SourceId.description:
Input source, formatted `kind:instance` by convention
(`gaze:webcam0`, `switch:ble0` — SPEC §4.A). The format is a
convention, not a parsing contract.

SpeakRequest.description:
`POST /voice/speak` request. Response: streamed audio
(`audio/ogg; codecs=opus`), first sample < 200 ms (SPEC §5.A).

prosody.description:
Prosody tag applied to the whole utterance (one commit = one tag,
D-6.3).

text.description: Text to vocalize. **P0 content** — never logged.

StyleProfile.description:
Style profile (SPEC §7.B: tutoiement ? humour ? façon de dire oui/non ?
expressions à soi ?). All fields optional: an empty style is valid.

expressions.description: Signature expressions, injected into the style prompt. **P0.**

humor.description: How their humor sounds, in their words (« pince-sans-rire »). **P0.**

informal_address.description: Prefers informal address (« tutoiement ») with close people.

yes_no_style.description: How they typically say yes/no (« ouais », « pas question »). **P0.**

SuggestAborted.description: Why a generation ended without a final list.

max_chars.description: Hard cap on suggestion length, characters.

SuggestEvent.description:
Events of the `/suggest` SSE stream (SPEC §5.A).

SSE mapping: the variant name is the SSE `event:` field, the payload is
the `data:` JSON. The canonical JSON form below (`{"event": …, "data":
…}`) is what the schema documents.

`non_exhaustive`: streams may gain event kinds; clients ignore unknown
events.

oneOf.description: Incremental text for suggestion `i` (append to previous deltas).
Terminal event: the complete, post-processed suggestion list.

suggestions.description:
Post-processed suggestions, best first. May be shorter than `n`
(an empty slot costs less than a bad suggestion, SPEC §7.A).

oneOf.description: Noisy/telegraphic fragment → full sentence in the user's style.

SuggestRequest.description:
`POST /sessions/{id}/suggest` request (SPEC §5.A).

Wire example:
`{"mode":"rephrase","draft":"veu eau frache ce soir","n":3,"slot":"main",`
`"constraints":{"max_chars":120,"register":"famille"},"style_profile":"default"}`

draft.description:
Current draft — tolerant to eye-typing noise by design.
**P0 content** — never logged.

n.description: Number of suggestions requested (UI guidance: 3 max, SPEC §7.A).

slot.description:
Cancellation slot: a new request on the same slot aborts the
previous one (it receives [`SuggestEvent::Aborted`]).

style_profile.description: Style profile to speak *like the person* (SPEC §5).

origin.description:
Where it came from — memory-sourced suggestions carry a discreet
marker in the UI (SPEC §5.B).

score.description:
Model confidence, higher is better (comparable within one response
only).

oneOf.description:
Short-circuited from a recurring phrase in personal memory
(no LLM call — SPEC §5.B).
Produced by the embedded n-gram fallback (LLM down, SPEC §2.C).
Origin added by a newer hub.

SurfaceId.description: A target surface (screen/window) declared by a UI (SPEC §4.A).

SwitchEvent.description:
A physical switch transition (SPEC §4.A).

Wire example: `{"k":"sw","t":123456789,"src":"switch:ble0","btn":1,"state":"down"}`

btn.description: Button index on the device, starting at 1.

t.description: Source timestamp, microseconds, monotonic per source.

SystemEvent.description: Hub → clients events on the `system` topic.

oneOf.description:
First frame after a WebSocket open: negotiation outcome
(see [`crate::ws`] for the connection contract).

topics.description: Topics actually granted (requested ∩ allowed by scope).

v.description: Protocol version retained by the hub.

oneOf.description:
A worker changed state — the explicit degradation chain of
« le clavier parle toujours » (SPEC §2.C, D-2.6).

restart_count.description: Restarts since hub boot (present when the supervisor restarts it).

oneOf.description:
ASR listening state changed. Every UI must show a visible indicator
while listening is on (SPEC §5.A — third-party privacy).

enabled.description: Whether the microphone pipeline is active.

label.description: Human-readable label (the letter of a key, the text of a suggestion).

prior.description:
Linguistic prior in `[0, 1]` modulating adaptive dwell and capped
magnetism (SPEC §4.C — never more than 40% of inter-target distance).

TargetId.description: A selectable target declared by a UI (SPEC §4.A).

TargetMap.description:
Full declaration of a UI's selectable targets for one surface
(`PUT /input/targets`, SPEC §4.A).

Wire example: `{"surface":"main","viewport":{"w":1920,"h":1080},"targets":[…]}`

targets.description: Complete list — replaces any previous declaration for this surface.

viewport.description: Pixel size of the surface viewport (target rects are expressed in it).

TargetMapPatch.description:
Incremental update of a surface's targets (WebSocket `input` topic).

Application order: `upsert` (insert or replace by id), then `remove`.
A `viewport` change invalidates nothing else — rects of untouched targets
are assumed already expressed in the new viewport.

upsert.description: Targets to insert or replace (matched by `id`).

viewport.description: New viewport size, if it changed.

TargetRole.description:
Semantic role of a target.

Unknown roles deserialize as [`TargetRole::Unknown`] so older hubs
tolerate newer UIs (forward compatibility).

oneOf.description: One of the (max 3, fixed-position) suggestion slots (SPEC §7.A).
Any other actionable control (PARLER, tabs, emergency — SPEC §7.A).
Role added by a newer peer; treated with default policies.

TimestampMicros.description:
Microseconds since an arbitrary epoch, monotonic **per source**
(SPEC §4.A).

Monotonicity is a stream property the hub enforces at ingestion; the type
itself only carries the value.

Topic.description:
A multiplexing topic (SPEC §2.A). Subscription is filtered by scope:
`display` receives `system` + composed text; `control` adds `input`…

oneOf.description: Input protocol: samples in, selection events out (SPEC §4.A).
Partner speech transcription (P2 — reserved, no frames in v1).
Push suggestions (P2 — reserved, no frames in v1).
Voice/TTS events (P2 — reserved, no frames in v1).
System state: degradations, listening indicator, hello (SPEC §2.C).

TurnSource.description: How a turn's text was produced.

oneOf.description: Transcribed from the partner's speech (consented ASR, §5.A).
Spoken through TTS by the user.

Operating-system fallback voice (SAPI / espeak-ng) — always
available (« une voix, toujours », SPEC §2.C).
Piper voice (stock or personal fine-tuning, D-6.1).
Personal cloned voice, quality stage (F5-TTS, GPU tiers).
Kind added by a newer hub.

WorkerKind.description:
An inference worker supervised by the hub (SPEC §2.C).

Unknown kinds deserialize as [`WorkerKind::Unknown`] (forward
compatibility).

oneOf.description: Worker kind added by a newer hub.

WorkerState.description: Worker lifecycle state (PLAN Phase 2.3 supervisor states).

oneOf.description: Not serving; supervisor is backing off before restart.

fluenceToken.description: Per-device revocable token with a scope (SPEC §2.A)
fluenceToken.name: X-Fluence-Token

info.description: API of the Fluence hub — a local-first communication platform for people with severe motor disabilities. One session = one conversation with a warm KV-cache; suggestions stream over SSE with per-slot cancellation; input flows through the FluenceInput protocol (WebSocket topics). Authentication: per-device revocable tokens with scopes (display/control/care/system). Operations are marked `x-fluence-stability: stable` (A1 core, frozen) or `experimental` (P2 domains, may change). Errors follow RFC 9457 problem+json with a stable machine-readable code catalogue.
info.summary: Local communication hub: input, acceleration, voice (SPEC §5.A)
info.title: Fluence hub API

post.summary: Obtain a journaled ASR consent token (explicit UI action)

put.summary: Declare the full target map of a surface

post.summary: Semantic forgetting: list candidates to confirm
Accept a learned candidate into memory

parameters.description: Draft prefix to condition the distribution on
parameters.name: prefix

get.summary: Next-character distribution on the warm KV

200.description: Server-sent event stream. The `event:` field is the variant tag, `data:` is the JSON payload (canonical form documented by the schema).

post.summary: Vocalize text (P0 scheduler priority, streamed Opus)
Pair a device (only while a pairing window is open)

101.description: WebSocket upgrade — frames are ServerFrame / ClientFrame (see components), first frame is system.hello
